ceiling_reason: Two NOT CHECKED security rows (auth-gating on chip endpoint), four PARTIAL rows including the critical 'live' real-time semantics ambiguity, one UNSUPPORTED row, and five documented gaps prevent a score above ~75. The proposal is buildable but requires auth confirmation, definition of 'live', and an end-to-end test before it is fully de-risked.

evidence.claim: A 'chip' UI component pattern already exists in the dashboard codebase and can be reused for the live status chip
evidence.perspective: Chip pattern is established; reuse is low-risk and avoids new component scaffolding
evidence.source_title: TopBar.tsx – existing chip usage in control panel

evidence.claim: A 'chip' UI component pattern already exists in the dashboard codebase and can be reused for the live status chip
evidence.perspective: Second confirmation of chip pattern; clone-and-feature-flag path is viable
evidence.source_title: HealthGrid.tsx – existing chip usage in health/status grid

evidence.claim: The overview page already renders chip-style elements, confirming a reusable pattern for the Mission Control panel
evidence.perspective: Third confirmation; chip is a first-class UI primitive in this codebase
evidence.source_title: overview/page.tsx – chip rendering in main dashboard

evidence.claim: Live pipeline status data is already being tracked server-side and can feed the chip
evidence.perspective: Cron-based triggers expose status fields but may not support sub-minute real-time updates; polling latency unconfirmed
evidence.source_title: cron_triggers.py – live and status references in pipeline scheduling

evidence.claim: A cron store exists that tracks pipeline state, providing a backend source for live status
evidence.perspective: Minimal chip implementation can read directly from cron_store without new backend; strongest backend evidence row
evidence.source_title: cron_store.py – live state storage for pipelines

evidence.claim: Observability tests for Mission Control already exist, reducing risk of undetected regressions
evidence.perspective: Safety net exists; rollback is testable
evidence.source_title: test_mission_control_observability.py – live status observability tests

evidence.claim: Linear pulse integration tracks status changes and could serve as a live data source for the chip
evidence.perspective: Linear is an external dependency; latency and availability make it a secondary, not primary, data source for a live chip
evidence.source_title: linear_pulse.py – status tracking via Linear integration

evidence.claim: The static frontend already references 'spec' and 'status', suggesting some pipeline status surface exists in the UI
evidence.perspective: Static HTML references are weak evidence; does not confirm dynamic status propagation to the Next.js dashboard
evidence.source_title: index.html – spec and status references in static frontend

evidence.claim: No evidence of auth-gating or role checks on the Mission Control panel chip endpoint; boundary risks are unverified
evidence.perspective: This is a security gap, not a supporting claim; auth boundary for the new status endpoint must be confirmed before ship
evidence.source_title: cron_triggers.py – status and live data exposed without confirmed auth layer

evidence.claim: Live status polling from the chip could expose internal pipeline state to unauthorized dashboard viewers
evidence.perspective: Auth boundary for HealthGrid chip is unconfirmed; same risk applies to proposed new chip
evidence.source_title: HealthGrid.tsx – chip renders status data with unknown auth boundary

evidence.claim: No secrets or credentials are referenced in the chip or status components reviewed
evidence.perspective: Positive security signal; chip layer does not handle credentials directly
evidence.source_title: TopBar.tsx – chip component with no visible secret handling

evidence.claim: Session recovery tests exist, suggesting auth session handling is tested but not confirmed for the new chip endpoint
evidence.perspective: Session recovery coverage is adjacent but does not close the auth-gating gap on the chip endpoint
evidence.source_title: test_session_recovery.py – session and status recovery coverage

evidence.claim: The proposal may be over-scoped if 'live' status requires real-time WebSocket or polling infrastructure not yet in place
evidence.perspective: Real-time semantics are undefined; if 'live' means WebSocket push, new infrastructure is required; if polling is acceptable, cron_store suffices
evidence.source_title: cron_triggers.py – cron-based triggers may not support true real-time live status

evidence.claim: Chip pattern is already used in multiple dashboard components, so adding another is low-risk and not over-scoped
evidence.perspective: Consistent with other SUPPORTED chip evidence; scope risk is low for the UI layer specifically
evidence.source_title: overview/page.tsx – precedent for chip reuse reduces scope risk

evidence.claim: Spec pipeline tests exist but do not confirm end-to-end live status propagation to the UI chip
evidence.perspective: Gap in end-to-end test coverage; backend-to-chip data flow is untested
evidence.source_title: test_sdk_phase2.py – spec pipeline tests without confirmed UI status propagation

evidence.claim: Specialised skill foundation tests reference 'spec' but do not validate Mission Control chip behavior
evidence.perspective: These tests are irrelevant to the chip; their existence neither supports nor undermines the proposal
evidence.source_title: test_specialised_skill_foundation.py – spec tests not covering chip UI

evidence.claim: The smallest reversible version is a static chip reading from an existing cron_store status field, with no new backend required
evidence.perspective: Strongly supports a low-risk MVP; aligns with RA-1109 surface-treatment prohibition by avoiding unnecessary new infrastructure
evidence.source_title: cron_store.py – existing status field usable for minimal chip implementation

evidence.claim: Existing HealthGrid chip pattern can be cloned and feature-flagged for a safe, reversible rollout
evidence.perspective: Feature-flag path is sound; reversibility is confirmed by existing test coverage
evidence.source_title: HealthGrid.tsx – chip component suitable for feature-flagged clone

evidence.claim: Mission Control observability tests provide a safety net for reverting the chip if it causes regressions
evidence.perspective: Rollback safety is confirmed; this is the strongest testability signal in the proposal
evidence.source_title: test_mission_control_observability.py – regression coverage for safe rollback

evidence.claim: Next.js config and ESLint config are in place, so the new chip component will be linted and built consistently
evidence.perspective: Standard build hygiene; not a differentiating factor but confirms no tooling gaps
evidence.source_title: next.config.ts – build pipeline supports new dashboard components

gaps: Auth boundary for the new chip's status endpoint is NOT CHECKED — must confirm the cron_store or cron_triggers status route is protected by the same auth middleware as other Mission Control routes before shipping

'Live' is undefined: proposal does not specify whether 'live' means WebSocket push, SSE, or polling interval — this ambiguity risks scope creep into new real-time infrastructure; must be pinned to a concrete mechanism (recommend: polling cron_store every 30s as MVP)

No end-to-end test covering the full path: cron_store status field → API route → chip render → correct label displayed; test_sdk_phase2.py and test_mission_control_observability.py are adjacent but do not close this gap
Proposal does not specify which exact field in cron_store maps to the chip label/colour states (e.g. idle, running, failed, succeeded) — data contract between backend and chip is unspecified
No mention of error/fallback state for the chip when the status endpoint is unreachable — UX for degraded connectivity is undefined
proposal: Add live status chip to Spec Pipeline Mission Control panel
